events.event_description: U.S. Legation to Norway separately established when Graves presented credentials to Norway independently, having previously been commissioned jointly to Sweden and Norway and resident at Stockholm.

evidence.quote: Charles Hinman Graves, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1905-03-08. Credentials presented: 1905-05-31. Functions with respect to Norway ceased 1906-08-06. Note: Commissioned to Sweden and Norway; resident at Stockholm. On November 14, 1905, Graves acknowledged instructions to exercise the functions of his office toward Norway separately.

events.event_description: Legation in Oslo closed, ending formal U.S. diplomatic presence in occupied Norway.

evidence.quote: Raymond Edwin Cox, Chargé d'Affaires ad interim. Legation Oslo closed on 1940-07-15.

events.event_description: U.S. Legation re-established near the Government of Norway in exile in England, with Schoenfeld making his initial call as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim.

evidence.quote: Rudolf E. Schoenfeld opened the Legation near the Government of Norway established in England, making his initial call as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim on August 2, 1940.

events.event_description: Legation elevated to Embassy when Biddle was promoted from Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary.

evidence.quote: Anthony Joseph Drexel Jr Biddle, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1941-02-11. Credentials presented: 1941-03-20. Promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary 1942-05-13.
